name: v0.8_supported_band_screened_closer_route_normalized_v2
purpose: Test whether v0.8 should replace a shipped full-damage, zero-reach ranged advance only when the strict route is both natively screened from the aimed target and strictly closer to that target, with production-compatible route normalization and fully observable route-validity metadata.

failedCandidate.reason: All 83 eligible comparisons in each arm failed closed because the producer rejected production paths whose first serialized cell was the source cell; treatment selected no strict decisions and produced no causal differences.

burnedSeedAllocation.disposition: The prior candidate, master, pilot lane, and all unused lanes reserved under that master are retired and must not be reused.

priorEvidencePolicy.use: The c28113b report, the earlier failed dominance pilot, and prior broad-overlay route subsets are forensic or hypothesis-generation evidence only.

sourceRequirement.candidate: A new clean pushed commit containing this protocol, the route-normalized screened-closer implementation, complete neutral route-validity telemetry, and focused production-shape tests.

selector.name: screened_closer
allConditionsRequired: Both decisions are distinct move_unit plus ordinary range_attack decisions.
Both decisions preserve the same declared target and visible aim edge, and both source-pinned retainedSignatureAfter booleans are true.
Both primary target divisors after movement equal 1.
Both reachable-threat counts after movement equal 0.
Strict and shipped targetDistanceBefore values are equal, finite, nonnegative integers.
Strict targetDistanceAfter is strictly less than shipped targetDistanceAfter and both are strictly less than targetDistanceBefore.
A living native-melee ally screens the strict destination from the aimed target.
No living native-melee ally screens the shipped destination from the aimed target.
The strict and shipped destination footprints differ.

telemetryBindings: Every strict moved decision serializes strictFromCell, strictToCell, strictDivisorBefore, and strictReachableThreatsBefore.
Every shipped moved decision serializes shippedFromCell, shippedToCell, and shippedDivisorBefore; those fields are null exactly for a structurally valid shipped shot-only fallback.
Strict and shipped moved decisions bind their serialized fromCell and toCell values to the same catalog metadata used by the producer comparator.
routeValidity.normalization: For each moved decision, accept either a path whose first cell is the first travel step or a path with exactly one optional leading cell equal to fromCell. If the first cell equals fromCell, remove exactly that one cell before validating travel. No additional occurrence serving as a source prefix is accepted.
normalizedTravel: Every fromCell, toCell, path cell, and target-footprint cell is a safe in-bounds integer grid cell.
Starting at fromCell, every normalized travel step has Chebyshev distance exactly one from the prior cell; zero-length and nonadjacent steps are invalid.
The last normalized travel cell equals toCell.
The executable move path endpoint equals the catalog toCell, and moveTargetCells is nonempty, unique, safe, and contains that same toCell anchor.
Strict and shipped fromCell values are equal because both catalogs describe the same actor at the same pre-divergence root.
beforeFieldConsistency: strictDivisorBefore and moved shippedDivisorBefore are finite positive powers of two and equal.
strictDivisorAfter is exactly 1 and strictly less than strictDivisorBefore.
A moved shipped divisorAfter is a finite positive power of two strictly less than shippedDivisorBefore.
strictReachableThreatsBefore is a finite nonnegative integer; strictReachableThreatsAfter remains exactly zero.
routeValidity.producerAnalyzerEquivalence: The producer and frozen analyzer apply the same normalization and validity predicates to the same serialized fields. A validity flag mismatch fails integrity; the analyzer must not infer omitted route-source or before fields.

selector.targetDistanceDefinition: Minimum pairwise Chebyshev distance between the complete shooter destination footprint and complete target footprint.
selector.screenDefinition: The guard is a living same-team native MELEE or MELEE_MAGIC unit, is strictly closer to the aimed target than the shooter destination, is within three Chebyshev cells of the shooter destination, and satisfies guardToTarget + shooterToGuard <= shooterToTarget + 1.
selector.fallback: Return the exact shipped decision object and publish shipped branch events whenever any condition is absent, equal, malformed, or inconsistent.
selector.malformedMetadata: Malformed or structurally inconsistent metadata fails closed to shipped and fails integrity. A legitimate shipped shot-only decision with null move metadata, or well-formed metadata that merely fails a qualification predicate, is valid filtered telemetry and does not fail integrity.

validitySemantics.strict: A comparison is emitted only from a strict proposal, so route-normalized strict move+shot, 1x improvement, zero threats, closer-than-current, native screen+guard, retained signature, and safe geometry are producer invariants; violating one is an integrity failure.
validitySemantics.shipped: A structurally valid shot-only fallback or route-normalized moved decision that is partial, threatened, non-closing, signature-changing, screened, or otherwise ineligible is valid filtered telemetry.
validitySemantics.relative: Strict-closer-than-shipped, shipped-unscreened, and distinct-route tests are qualification predicates; failing one filters without an integrity failure.

selector.oldDominanceReasons: no_shipped_advance, lower_divisor, and lower_reachable_threats are not enabled by this candidate.
selector.treatment: v0.8 selects a screened_closer strict proposal; v0.8s remains shipped.
selector.control: At every matched pre-divergence root, v0.8 computes identical shipped-first and strict-second catalogs, objective comparison telemetry, and RNG tails but returns shipped. Later roots may differ after a treatment action changes the trajectory; v0.8s remains shipped.

seedAllocation.masterSelection: Fresh randomly generated master fixed and recorded before any route-normalized-v2 outcome access.

disjointnessProof.method: The odd stride is invertible modulo 2^32. Each listed prior A/B lane base, including every retired c28113b lane base, was converted to this master's pair-index cycle and assigned a deliberately dominating 1000000-cluster forward span before comparison with the conservative new envelope from index 0 through 49999.
disjointnessProof.dominatingPriorSpanBasis: Campaign-history assumption: no known listed prior campaign allocated one million pair-seed clusters from a single base. This also covers the earlier requested one-million-game study, which is at most 500000 paired clusters. There is no hashed exhaustive historical lane registry, so this is deliberately not described as a complete machine-verifiable proof.

statistics.causalContrast: Treatment minus matched screened-closer selector-off control.
statistics.cluster: Independent pair-seed cluster containing both physical-seat swaps.
statistics.confidenceInterval: Two-sided 95% Student-t interval over pair-seed cluster means.

exposureCounting.sourceArm: Treatment only; control neutral telemetry is never pooled or double-counted. Objective fields must match after normalization only at causally matched pre-divergence roots.
exposureCounting.strictEligible: All treatment neutral screened-closer comparison events emitted before a13 retention.
exposureCounting.screenedCloserProposals: Treatment neutral comparisons with dominant=true before the selector-off control is applied.
exposureCounting.retainedActualDifferences: Treatment screened_closer decisions that survive a13 and differ executably from shipped.
exposureCounting.retainedActualDifferenceGames: Unique treatment game records containing at least one retained executable screened_closer difference.

perComparison: strict and shipped executable action summaries
same declared target and visible aim edge
shippedFromCell, shippedToCell, and shippedDivisorBefore for a moved shipped decision; all are null exactly for a structurally valid shot-only fallback
raw strict and shipped move paths retained so the frozen analyzer can remove exactly one optional leading source cell and validate nonempty normalized travel
source-pinned retained-signature booleans computed from exact first-hit, affected-unit, and divisor arrays
strict divisorAfter, reachableThreatsAfter, target distances, screen, guard, and signature proof fields
shipped divisor, threat, distance, screen, guard, and signature proof fields are nullable exactly for a structurally valid shot-only fallback; otherwise they are typed and internally consistent

deterministic strict screeningGuardId; a moved shipped decision has internally consistent targetScreenedAfter and screeningGuardId, with null shipped guard required for dominant or selected screened_closer proposals
physical seat, actor, target, lap, retained, selected, and reason
telemetryRequirements.selectedStrictEvents: Treatment only when screened_closer is true and selector is enabled.
telemetryRequirements.actualDifferenceDefinition: Strict and shipped completed decision summaries differ; identical actions never count as causal exposure.

integrityGates: Frozen clean pushed source head/tree and recomputed manifest fingerprints.
Complete unique game IDs and exact pair-seed formula.
Treatment/control geometry identical and only the screened-closer selector-off key differs.
Shipped-first then strict catalog order with the exact same cached route catalog and equal RNG tails.
Control selects zero strict decisions; at causally matched pre-divergence roots, objective catalog/comparison/RNG fields match after normalizing selected, retained, and the control environment.
Every moved comparison serializes its source, destination, divisor-before, and required strict threats-before fields; producer and analyzer route-validity results agree exactly.
Each moved path accepts at most one optional leading source cell, has nonempty normalized travel, is cell-by-cell adjacent, and has mutually consistent endpoint, toCell, and destination footprint.
Every selected decision satisfies every serialized screened-closer condition and has a different endpoint.
Pinned source plus focused production-PathHelper tests prove both accepted path encodings, retained-signature computation, and target-screen computation.
The declared action target and visible aim edge match across strict and shipped, and both retained-signature booleans are true.

Malformed, nonfinite, negative, omitted-required, or structurally inconsistent metadata fails closed and fails integrity; legitimate absent shipped move metadata and well-formed ineligible values are filtered without an integrity failure.
Exact shipped decision object and branch telemetry are preserved on every filter.
Every outcome, lap, or Armageddon difference has a retained screened-closer causal decision.
No-proposal games have identical core records; normalized records differ only by neutral diagnostics.
Zero rejected actions, stuck endings, turn caps, or other non-elimination endings.

phaseOrderAndNoPeek.duringPhase: Inspect process liveness and exact record counts only. Do not open logs, records, summaries, result lines, or one-arm statistics until both arms finish.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.remoteBarrier: For remotePrimary, all four treatment and all four control shards must exit successfully with exact counts before any of the eight logs, records, summaries, manifests, or outcome statistics are opened.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.prePeekFailure: Discard both arms and restart both on the same lane.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.prelaunchReceipt: Before every phase, create a read-only JSON receipt outside all output directories containing candidate head/tree, protocol SHA-256, analyzer SHA-256 and self-test result, exact commands using --prelaunch-receipt with that receipt path, geometry, seed lane, output directories, and creation time. Compute its SHA-256 and publish that exact digest in the user-visible transcript before any phase artifact is created. The runner must hash the receipt bytes into every experiment manifest/fingerprint; the analyzer must verify that binding and require manifest/records/summary filesystem creation or modification times later than the receipt.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.priorReceipt: Every phase after pilot additionally requires the immediately preceding passing report and its SHA-256, published before the next prelaunch receipt and artifacts are created.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.analyzerMutation: Any analyzer byte change after prelaunch-receipt publication invalidates the phase. Before any outcome peek, discard all phase arms and restart with a newly published receipt; after any outcome peek, stop and create a new candidate and seed master.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.gateFailure: Stop the candidate. Do not launch the next phase.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.underexposure: Stop as underexposed; never relax the screen or distance rule and never extend adaptively.
phaseOrderAndNoPeek.codeConfigThresholdChange: Creates a new candidate with a new seed master and preregistration.

promotionMeaning.defaultReadiness: Not authorized without a separately preregistered bounded operational phase.
promotionMeaning.globalNearZeroArmageddonClaim: Not tested by this feature-specific protocol.
promotionMeaning.projectileEndpointClaim: Out of scope; renderer endpoint correctness requires separate client integration evidence.
